research_topics: HMM regime detection for signal selection
Walk-forward optimization for continuous parameter tuning

findings.finding: Factor weights dynamically adjusted based on rolling Information Coefficient (IC = correlation between signal and future returns). When a signals recent IC declines, its weight automatically decreases toward zero. Empirical results show IC-weighted strategies outperform equal-weighted.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL - our system uses equal voting (1 signal = 1 vote). Signals with 40% accuracy get same vote weight as 74% signals. IC-based weighting would naturally downweight failing signals like trend (40.3%) and upweight funding (74.2%).

findings.topic: HMM Regime Detection + Regime-Filtered Signal Selection
findings.finding: HMM with 2-3 states trained on returns+volatility. Different signal subsets activated per regime. Regime-adaptive position sizing reduces in high-vol regimes.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH - we detect regime but dont use it to SELECT which signals to trust. Trend signal is 40.3% overall but 61.6% recently - likely trending regime where it works.

findings.finding: Bull and Bear agents debate with structured rounds. TradingAgents v0.2.3 supports Claude 4.6. Apex Quant uses 3 personas. Both show improved Sharpe ratio vs single-agent.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM - our Layer 2 already uses bull/bear debate but single-pass. Could add structured 2-round debate.

findings.finding: PCA compresses correlated signals, reducing dimensionality. Filter-based feature selection removes redundant features. RL-based alpha weighting outperforms equal-weighted.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH - 32 active signals, many likely correlated (trend+EMA+heikin_ashi, momentum+oscillators+momentum_factors). Pruning reduces noise.

findings.finding: Position size = dollar_risk / (ATR * multiplier). ADX modifies multiplier. Q1 2026 study: ATR-based risk management reduced drawdowns by 43% vs fixed-lot.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH - we use fixed budget allocations. ATR-based sizing would naturally reduce positions in volatile markets.

findings.finding: Sentiment scores often lack robust predictive power for real-time trading. Continuous recalibration needed. GNN-based models improve over traditional NLP.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL - sentiment crashed from 46.5% to 33.8% recent 3h. CryptoBERT may need recalibration or gating.

findings.finding: Rolling-window: optimize in-sample, test out-of-sample, slide forward. Prevents overfitting. Best practice for production trading systems.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM - signal parameters are static. WFO could re-optimize periodically. High implementation cost for marginal gains.

recommended_improvements.title: IC-based dynamic signal weighting (replace equal voting)
recommended_improvements.description: Compute rolling 7-day IC for each signal. Use IC as vote weight instead of equal 1.0. Auto-downweight failing signals.

recommended_improvements.title: Gate sentiment signal when recent accuracy < 40%
recommended_improvements.description: Auto-gate sentiment when 7-day accuracy drops below 40%. Currently at 33.8% - actively harmful.

recommended_improvements.title: Signal correlation matrix and redundancy detection
recommended_improvements.description: Compute pairwise correlation of signal outputs over 30-day window. Warn when correlation > 0.8.

recommended_improvements.title: ATR-based position sizing
recommended_improvements.description: Replace fixed budget with ATR-scaled sizing. Regime-adjusted multipliers.

recommended_improvements.title: Recency-weighted accuracy for auto-gating
recommended_improvements.description: Use 70% recent 7d + 30% all-time for gate decision instead of all-time only.
